non-null result."},{"id":"c","text":"The subclass logs extra information before returning."},{"id":"d","text":"The subclass delegates the call to the base class implementation."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q6","text":"ST19-1: Which SOLID principle most directly improves the testability of a class?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP — because classes closed for modification are easier to test."},{"id":"b","text":"DIP — because depending on abstractions allows test doubles to be injected in place of real dependencies."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP — because small interfaces have fewer methods to mock."},{"id":"d","text":"SRP — because smaller classes have fewer edge cases to test."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q7","text":"ST08-5: Why does ISP indirectly improve unit testability?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Smaller interfaces require fewer mock setup lines, making tests simpler and more focused."},{"id":"b","text":"ISP eliminates the need for mocking entirely."},{"id":"c","text":"Smaller interfaces run faster in the test runner."},{"id":"d","text":"ISP forces all test doubles to extend the same base class."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q8","text":"ST07-8: A developer defines `IPrintable`, `IScannable`, and `IFaxable` separately. A laser printer class implements all three. An inkjet class implements only `IPrintable` and `IScannable`. This design satisfies:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"ISP — each interface represents a single role; implementors adopt only relevant roles."},{"id":"b","text":"OCP — the printer hierarchy is open for extension."},{"id":"c","text":"SRP — each printer class has a single responsibility."},{"id":"d","text":"LSP — the inkjet is substitutable for the laser C#"},{"id":"d","text":"MyEvent!.Invoke(this, args)"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q10","text":"ST17-8: Which SOLID principle violation is most commonly associated with the 'Shotgun conceptual change affects many of them."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP — too many interface methods require updating."},{"id":"d","text":"DIP — too many concrete dependencies need object used in both tests and the live system."},{"id":"b","text":"A test double that records calls made to it and can be verified to have been called correctly."},{"id":"c","text":"A simplified real implementation with no side effects."},{"id":"d","text":"An object that returns hardcoded data without recording calls."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q13","text":"ST06-12: A subclass This subclass:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Violates LSP by strengthening the precondition."},{"id":"b","text":"Complies with LSP by weakening the precondition (accepting more than the base requires)."},{"id":"c","text":"Violates OCP by modifying the base class contract."},{"id":"d","text":"Violates ISP by implementing an extra capability."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q14","text":"ST18-6: 'Introduce Parameter Object' refactoring is used when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A method has no parameters and needs one added."},{"id":"b","text":"A group of parameters always appear together in multiple methods and can be grouped into a cohesive object."},{"id":"c","text":"A parameter is used only once and should be inlined."},{"id":"d","text":"A method returns multiple values that should be grouped."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q15","text":"ST05-14: Which type of test failure most clearly reveals an LSP violation in a class hierarchy?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A test that fails only when the concrete subtype is used in place of the base type."},{"id":"b","text":"A test that fails due to a compile error."},{"id":"c","text":"A test that fails due to a timeout."},{"id":"d","text":"A test that fails because the database is unavailable."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q16","text":"ST07-9: In C# 8.0+, default interface methods can be used to extend interfaces without breaking existing implementations. How does this support ISP?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It encourages merging all interfaces into one."},{"id":"b","text":"It allows optional extensions to be added to role interfaces without forcing all implementors to add the method."},{"id":"c","text":"It removes the need for ISP entirely."},{"id":"d","text":"It forces all classes to implement the default method."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q17","text":"ST09-3: Which of the following code snippets demonstrates a DIP violation?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A service class that accepts ILogger in its constructor."},{"id":"b","text":"A service class that instantiates `new SqlDatabase()` directly in its constructor."},{"id":"c","text":"A service class that accepts IDatabase in its constructor."},{"id":"d","text":"A service class registered in a DI container."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q18","text":"ST19-3: A 'Stub' test double, in contrast to a Mock, is primarily used to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Verify that specific methods were called during a test."},{"id":"b","text":"Provide canned responses to method calls, controlling indirect inputs to the system under test."},{"id":"c","text":"Record all calls and replay them later."},{"id":"d","text":"Replace the entire production database with an in-memory version."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q19","text":"ST17-9: 'Data Clumps' is a code smell where:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A class has too few fields."},{"id":"b","text":"The same group of fields or parameters appear together in multiple places and should be extracted into a class."},{"id":"c","text":"Static data is shared across multiple classes."},{"id":"d","text":"A collection is passed where a scalar value would suffice."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q20","text":"ST08-6: A `IContentService` interface has methods: `GetArticle()`, `PostComment()`, `DeleteComment()`, `ModerateComment()`. A guest user's class should only call `GetArticle()`. ISP suggests:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The guest class implements all four methods, throwing exceptions for three."},{"id":"b","text":"Split into IReadContent and IManageContent so guest users depend only on IReadContent."},{"id":"c","text":"Add an IsGuest property to IContentService."},{"id":"d","text":"Make all methods optional using default implementations."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q21","text":"ST15-12: In C#, what does unsubscribing from an event with `-=` prevent?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The event from being raised at all."},{"id":"b","text":"Memory leaks caused by the publisher holding a reference to the subscriber, preventing garbage collection."},{"id":"c","text":"The event from being raised more than once."},{"id":"d","text":"The subscriber from being registered a second time."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q22","text":"ST16-11: Which LINQ method is commonly used inside a repository's GetAll() implementation with Entity Framework Core?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"context.Set<T>().SaveChanges()"},{"id":"b","text":"context.Set<T>().ToListAsync()"},{"id":"c","text":"context.Set<T>().Attach()"},{"id":"d","text":"context.Set<T>().Entry()"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q23","text":"ST06-13: A `BankAccount.Withdraw()` base method guarantees balance will never go negative. A subclass `OverdraftAccount` allows negative balances. Is this an LSP violation?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"No — overdraft is a valid business rule."},{"id":"b","text":"Yes — the subclass weakens the invariant that balance >= 0, breaking the base class contract for code that relies on that guarantee."},{"id":"c","text":"No — as long as the method signature is the same."},{"id":"d","text":"Yes — but only if the overdraft feature is enabled."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q24","text":"ST18-7: 'Replace Magic Number with Symbolic Constant' is a refactoring that primarily improves:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Runtime performance."},{"id":"b","text":"Code readability and maintainability by giving meaningful names to unexplained numeric literals."},{"id":"c","text":"Test coverage metrics."},{"id":"d","text":"Memory usage."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q25","text":"ST09-4: Which of the following demonstrates a correctly DIP-compliant service registration?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OrderService environments."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q28","text":"ST07-10: The principle 'clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use' was formulated Martin (Uncle Bob)"},{"id":"c","text":"Erich Gamma"},{"id":"d","text":"Martin Fowler"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q29","text":"ST05-15: Which of the following changes to a base class method's return type is LSP-safe in a language that supports covariant return types?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Returning a less derived type than the base class."},{"id":"b","text":"Returning a more derived type (narrower type) that still satisfies the base class contract."},{"id":"c","text":"Returning void where the base returns a value."},{"id":"d","text":"Returning a completely unrelated type."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s05_q30","text":"ST18-8: 'Consolidate Conditional Expression' refactoring is used when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A single condition has too many operands."},{"id":"b","text":"Multiple conditions all lead to the same result and can be combined into a single clearly named condition or method."},{"id":"c","text":"A switch statement has too many cases."},{"id":"d","text":"A conditional expression is nested inside a loop."}],"correctOptionId":"b"}]}$$::jsonb) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

INSERT INTO papers (id, data) VALUES ('qp_cs_s06', $${"id":"qp_cs_s06","title":"C# SOLID — Paper 6","description":"Covers LSP refactoring, ISP concepts, ISP refactoring, DIP concepts, DIP implementation, Repository Pattern, Code Smells & SOLID Violations, Refactoring Techniques, Unit Testing & SOLID, and SOLID Real C# Scenarios.","createdAt":"2026-02-26T09:00:00Z","timer":{"enabled":true,"durationSeconds":2700,"autoSubmit":true},"questions":[{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q1","text":"ST10-1: Constructor injection in C# DIP implementation means:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The class creates its own dependencies inside the constructor using `new`."},{"id":"b","text":"Dependencies are provided to a class through its constructor parameters as abstractions (interfaces)."},{"id":"c","text":"The constructor calls a static factory to resolve dependencies."},{"id":"d","text":"Dependencies are injected after construction using setter properties."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q2","text":"ST20-1: Given the code snippet: `public class OrderService { private readonly EmailService _email = new EmailService(); }` — which SOLID principle is violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SRP — OrderService should not have any fields."},{"id":"b","text":"DIP — OrderService depends directly on a concrete EmailService rather than an abstraction."},{"id":"c","text":"OCP — EmailService cannot be extended."},{"id":"d","text":"ISP — EmailService has too many methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q3","text":"ST16-12: When implementing the Repository pattern with Entity Framework Core, which statement is most accurate?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"DbContext already acts as a repository, so adding a separate repository layer is always unnecessary."},{"id":"b","text":"Wrapping DbContext in a repository interface allows swapping EF Core for a different ORM or a fake in tests without changing business logic."},{"id":"c","text":"Repositories should include DbContext as a public property."},{"id":"d","text":"Repository methods should always return IQueryable<T> to push all query logic to callers."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q4","text":"ST08-7: In C#, explicit interface implementation is used to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Make all interface methods public on the class."},{"id":"b","text":"Implement an interface method without exposing it publicly on the class, so it is only accessible via the interface reference."},{"id":"c","text":"Allow the class to implement the same method name for two interfaces with different signatures simultaneously."},{"id":"d","text":"Both b and c."}],"correctOptionId":"d"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q5","text":"ST09-5: Which of the following is a direct consequence of violating DIP by instantiating concrete classes inside high-level modules?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The code compiles faster."},{"id":"b","text":"High-level modules become tightly coupled to low-level details, making substitution and unit testing difficult."},{"id":"c","text":"The program uses less memory at runtime."},{"id":"d","text":"The high-level module gains extra capabilities from the low-level module."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q6","text":"ST19-5: When SOLID principles are followed, why does mocking become easier?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Because mock frameworks only work with sealed classes."},{"id":"b","text":"Because dependencies are represented as interfaces or abstract types, which mocking frameworks can substitute easily."},{"id":"c","text":"Because SOLID-compliant classes always have public setters."},{"id":"d","text":"Because mocking is not needed when SOLID principles are applied."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q7","text":"ST06-14: When a derived class raises an exception type that the base class does not declare, this can violate LSP because:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Callers expect only the exceptions defined by the base class contract; unexpected exceptions break substitutability."},{"id":"b","text":"Exceptions are always LSP violations regardless of context."},{"id":"c","text":"The derived class should never throw any exceptions."},{"id":"d","text":"This is not an LSP issue; exception types are irrelevant to substitutability."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q8","text":"ST20-2: A `Logger` class has methods: `LogInfo()`, `LogWarning()`, `LogError()`, `SendEmail()`, `WriteToDatabase()`. Which principle is most clearly violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP"},{"id":"b","text":"SRP — Logger has more than one reason to change (logging format, email transport, database schema)."},{"id":"c","text":"LSP"},{"id":"d","text":"ISP"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q9","text":"ST07-11: Which of the following correctly refactors a fat `IVehicle` interface (Drive, Fly, Sail) following ISP?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Add default implementations for Fly and Sail in IVehicle."},{"id":"b","text":"Split into IDriveable, IFlyable, and ISailable; a Car implements only IDriveable."},{"id":"c","text":"Mark Fly and Sail as optional using attributes."},{"id":"d","text":"Create a Vehicle base class with virtual methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q10","text":"ST17-11: 'Message Chains' (a.b().c().d()) is a code smell related to which principle?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"DIP — too many concrete dependencies."},{"id":"b","text":"Law of Demeter — a class should only talk to its immediate collaborators, not navigate deep object graphs."},{"id":"c","text":"LSP — the chain may break substitutability."},{"id":"d","text":"OCP — the chain cannot be extended."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q11","text":"ST10-2: The `IServiceCollection` extension method `AddTransient<TService, TImplementation>()` in ASP.NET Core registers a service so that:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"One instance is shared for the lifetime of the application."},{"id":"b","text":"A new instance is created every time the service is requested."},{"id":"c","text":"One instance is shared per HTTP request."},{"id":"d","text":"The implementation is resolved lazily on first use only."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q12","text":"ST18-9: 'Decompose Conditional' refactoring involves:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Removing all if-statements from a class."},{"id":"b","text":"Extracting the condition, then-part, and else-part of a complex if-else into well-named methods to improve clarity."},{"id":"c","text":"Combining multiple conditions into a single boolean expression."},{"id":"d","text":"Replacing conditions with try-catch blocks."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q13","text":"ST09-6: A 'Service Locator' is sometimes contrasted with DIP-based injection. What is the key criticism of Service Locator?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It is too slow at runtime."},{"id":"b","text":"It hides dependencies inside method bodies rather than declaring them explicitly, making them harder to discover and test."},{"id":"c","text":"It only supports singleton lifetimes."},{"id":"d","text":"It cannot work with interfaces."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q14","text":"ST19-6: A 'Spy' test double differs from a Mock in that:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A Spy provides canned responses, while a Mock throws exceptions."},{"id":"b","text":"A Spy wraps a real object and records calls made to it, while a Mock is entirely fabricated."},{"id":"c","text":"A Spy is set up before the test; a Mock is set up after."},{"id":"d","text":"A Spy can only be used with static methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q15","text":"ST20-3: A `ShapeRenderer` class has a method with `if (shape is Circle) { ... } else if (shape is Rectangle) { ... }`. Which principle does this violate?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"DIP"},{"id":"b","text":"OCP — adding a new shape requires modifying ShapeRenderer."},{"id":"c","text":"LSP"},{"id":"d","text":"ISP"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q16","text":"ST06-15: Which of the following statements about LSP and interfaces is correct?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Interfaces cannot be involved in LSP violations because they have no implementation."},{"id":"b","text":"Implementations of an interface must honour the behavioral contract implied by the interface, not just its method signatures."},{"id":"c","text":"Any class that compiles with an interface is automatically LSP compliant."},{"id":"d","text":"LSP only applies to abstract class hierarchies, not interface hierarchies."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q17","text":"ST10-3: In ASP.NET Core, when a service is registered with `AddSingleton<T>()`, its instance is:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Created once per HTTP request and shared within that request."},{"id":"b","text":"Created once for the application lifetime and shared across all requests and users."},{"id":"c","text":"Created every time it is requested."},{"id":"d","text":"Created only on the first HTTP request."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q18","text":"ST17-12: 'Parallel Inheritance Hierarchies' is a code smell where:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Two completely independent inheritance trees exist in the same project."},{"id":"b","text":"Every time you add a subclass in one hierarchy, you must add a corresponding subclass in another hierarchy, indicating tight coupling."},{"id":"c","text":"Two classes inherit from the same base class."},{"id":"d","text":"A hierarchy has more than five levels of inheritance."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q19","text":"ST08-8: A `IRepository<T>` interface includes `GetById`, `GetAll`, `Add`, `Update`, `Delete`, and `ExecuteRawSql`. A reporting service only needs `GetAll`. ISP suggests:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The reporting service implements all methods, throwing exceptions for unused ones."},{"id":"b","text":"Split off an IReadRepository<T> with just GetById and GetAll so read-only consumers depend on a narrower contract."},{"id":"c","text":"Add an IsReadOnly flag to IRepository<T>."},{"id":"d","text":"Make all write methods optional using attributes."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q20","text":"ST19-7: What does 'Arrange, Act, Assert' (AAA) mean in the context of unit testing?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Authenticate, Authorize, Audit."},{"id":"b","text":"Set up the test conditions (Arrange), execute the unit under test (Act), then verify the outcome (Assert)."},{"id":"c","text":"Add, Apply, Assess code changes."},{"id":"d","text":"Abstract, Aggregate, Annotate test cases."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q21","text":"ST20-4: A class `EmailSender` has a public method `Send()` that constructs an `SmtpClient` internally. What is the DIP-compliant refactoring?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Make SmtpClient a static field of EmailSender."},{"id":"b","text":"Introduce an IEmailTransport interface and inject it into EmailSender's constructor so the transport mechanism can be swapped or mocked."},{"id":"c","text":"Subclass SmtpClient inside EmailSender."},{"id":"d","text":"Move the SmtpClient construction into a separate private method."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q22","text":"ST09-7: In a DIP-compliant architecture, which layer typically defines the interfaces (abstractions)?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The infrastructure layer (e.g., data access)."},{"id":"b","text":"The high-level domain/application layer, so that low-level infrastructure depends on the domain's abstractions."},{"id":"c","text":"The presentation layer."},{"id":"d","text":"A shared utilities library."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q23","text":"ST10-4: Which of the following is a valid way to implement DIP without a DI container in C#?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Using `new` inside every class that needs a dependency."},{"id":"b","text":"Manually constructing the object graph at the application's composition root, passing abstractions via constructors."},{"id":"c","text":"Using static factory methods within each dependency class."},{"id":"d","text":"Using reflection to discover and instantiate all dependencies at startup."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q24","text":"ST16-13: In the Repository pattern, what is the preferred return type for a method that might or might not find an entity?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Always returning a default-constructed entity to avoid null."},{"id":"b","text":"Returning `T?` (nullable reference type) or a Result<T> type to explicitly communicate the absence of a value."},{"id":"c","text":"Throwing a NotFoundException if the entity is not found."},{"id":"d","text":"Returning an empty List<T> even for single-entity queries."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q25","text":"ST07-12: Which of the following statements about ISP and cohesion is correct?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"ISP reduces cohesion by splitting methods across too many interfaces."},{"id":"b","text":"ISP promotes high cohesion by ensuring each interface groups only closely related methods that a specific type of client needs."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP and cohesion are unrelated concerns."},{"id":"d","text":"High cohesion leads to fat interfaces that violate ISP."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q26","text":"ST20-5: A `UserController` directly instantiates `new UserRepository()` and `new EmailService()`. Which principles are violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP and LSP"},{"id":"b","text":"DIP and SRP — it depends on concrete types and mixes controller and service-creation concerns."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP and LSP"},{"id":"d","text":"OCP and ISP"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q27","text":"ST19-8: Why does a class that violates SRP tend to have low unit testability?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SRP violations cause compilation errors that prevent testing."},{"id":"b","text":"Mixed concerns mean the class has many hard-coded dependencies, making it hard to isolate and test any single behavior."},{"id":"c","text":"SRP violations always cause NullReferenceExceptions in test environments."},{"id":"d","text":"SRP-violating classes cannot be mocked."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q28","text":"ST18-10: 'Introduce Null Object' refactoring replaces null checks with:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Throwing ArgumentNullException everywhere null was expected."},{"id":"b","text":"A special object implementing the interface with do-nothing or default behavior, eliminating null reference checks."},{"id":"c","text":"A nullable wrapper type such as Nullable<T>."},{"id":"d","text":"An optional parameter with a default value of null."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q29","text":"ST06-16: An abstract class `Animal` has method `MakeSound()`. A `Fish` subclass inherits `Animal` and throws `NotSupportedException` from `MakeSound()`. The best LSP fix is:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Wrap the call in a try-catch in all callers."},{"id":"b","text":"Remove Fish from the Animal hierarchy; use composition or a separate ISoundMaker interface implemented only by sound-making animals."},{"id":"c","text":"Make Animal sealed so Fish cannot inherit from it."},{"id":"d","text":"Add an CanMakeSound boolean property to Animal."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s06_q30","text":"ST20-6: In C#, which code pattern indicates a violation of both OCP and SRP in a single method?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A method that delegates to an injected strategy object."},{"id":"b","text":"A method that both validates input AND applies business rules AND formats output using a chain of if-else statements that grow with every new feature."},{"id":"c","text":"A method that returns a result from an injected repository."},{"id":"d","text":"A method that calls two collaborating services injected via DI."}],"correctOptionId":"b"}]}$$::jsonb) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

persistence mechanism each require editing InvoiceManager."},{"id":"c","text":"Two — business logic and technical concerns."},{"id":"d","text":"Zero — the class is small enough to be SRP injection"},{"id":"d","text":"Service locator"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q3","text":"ST19-9: In a SOLID-compliant codebase, which of the following most accurately describes why doubles for real implementations, isolating the unit under test."},{"id":"c","text":"Interfaces reduce the number of test cases needed."},{"id":"d","text":"Interfaces automatically generate unit tests."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q4","text":"ST08-9: The CQRS (Command Query Responsibility Segregation) pattern is a high-level application of which SOLID principle?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"LSP — substituting queries with commands."},{"id":"b","text":"ISP — separating read (query) and write (command) interfaces so consumers depend only on what they use."},{"id":"c","text":"OCP — commands cannot be modified."},{"id":"d","text":"DIP — commands depend on abstractions."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q5","text":"ST09-8: Which of the Dependency Injection is the principle."},{"id":"b","text":"Dependency Inversion is the principle of depending on abstractions; Dependency Injection is a technique for fulfilling that principle at runtime."},{"id":"c","text":"They are the same concept."},{"id":"d","text":"Dependency Inversion reverses the call order of methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q6","text":"ST20-7: The following interface is defined: `interface IWorker { void Work(); void Eat(); void Sleep(); }` A `RobotWorker` class throws exceptions on `Eat()` and `Sleep()`. Which principle is violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP"},{"id":"b","text":"ISP — RobotWorker is forced to implement methods irrelevant to its role."},{"id":"c","text":"DIP"},{"id":"d","text":"LSP"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q7","text":"ST17-13: 'Inappropriate Intimacy' is a code smell where:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A class is too tightly coupled to a framework."},{"id":"b","text":"Two classes depend on each other's private internals, creating high coupling and fragile design."},{"id":"c","text":"A class has too many public methods."},{"id":"d","text":"A method has too many side effects."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q8","text":"ST10-5: In the Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection framework, the `IServiceProvider` interface is used to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Define which services should be registered."},{"id":"b","text":"Resolve services from the container at runtime, either directly or via constructor injection."},{"id":"c","text":"Register services with their lifetimes."},{"id":"d","text":"Validate that all registered services can be constructed."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q9","text":"ST18-11: 'Collapse Hierarchy' refactoring is appropriate when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A class hierarchy has too many levels and some levels add no distinct behavior."},{"id":"b","text":"Two sibling classes should be merged into one larger class."},{"id":"c","text":"An abstract class should become a concrete class."},{"id":"d","text":"An interface should be replaced with an abstract class."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q10","text":"ST07-13: A developer must refactor an `IAnimal` interface that has grown too large. Which principles are violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP and ISP"},{"id":"b","text":"SRP and DIP — multiple responsibilities and direct dependency on file system and database."},{"id":"c","text":"LSP and OCP"},{"id":"d","text":"ISP and LSP"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q14","text":"ST09-9: An application layer class `OrderService` depends on `IEmailSender` (interface). The `SmtpEmailSender` class implements `IEmailSender`. This demonstrates DIP because:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OrderService (high-level) depends on IEmailSender (abstraction), and SmtpEmailSender (low-level) also depends on IEmailSender (abstraction)."},{"id":"b","text":"SmtpEmailSender depends on OrderService."},{"id":"c","text":"IEmailSender depends on SmtpEmailSender's concrete methods."},{"id":"d","text":"OrderService creates SmtpEmailSender internally."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q15","text":"ST19-10: In TDD (Test-Driven Development), how do SOLID principles emerge naturally?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Tests enforce SOLID compliance at compile time."},{"id":"b","text":"Writing tests first forces you to design loosely-coupled, single-responsibility classes that are easy to construct and verify in isolation."},{"id":"c","text":"TDD generates SOLID-compliant code automatically."},{"id":"d","text":"TDD requires classes to implement many interfaces, naturally enforcing ISP."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q16","text":"ST08-10: An `INotificationService` interface has methods: `SendEmail()`, `SendSms()`, `SendPush()`, `SendFax()`. A client only needs to send emails. ISP-compliant refactoring would:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Create an IEmailNotification interface with only SendEmail(), letting the client depend on it."},{"id":"b","text":"Add an EmailOnly flag to INotificationService."},{"id":"c","text":"Mark unused methods with [Obsolete]."},{"id":"d","text":"Move SendEmail() to a static helper class."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q17","text":"ST17-14: 'Comments' can be a code smell when they are used to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Explain why a non-obvious decision was made."},{"id":"b","text":"Compensate for poorly named variables, methods, or classes — indicating the code itself is unclear."},{"id":"c","text":"Document public API methods."},{"id":"d","text":"Add copyright notices to source files."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q18","text":"ST10-6: The `IServiceCollection.AddScoped<T>()` method in ASP.NET Core creates one instance per:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Application lifetime."},{"id":"b","text":"HTTP request (or DI scope)."},{"id":"c","text":"Method call."},{"id":"d","text":"Thread."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q19","text":"ST20-9: A `PaymentProcessor` class has a method `Process(string type)` containing `if (type == \"PayPal\") ... else if (type == \"Stripe\") ...`. Which refactoring applies OCP?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Add more else-if branches and add comments explaining each."},{"id":"b","text":"Introduce an IPaymentStrategy interface with PayPalStrategy and StripeStrategy implementations; inject the chosen strategy."},{"id":"c","text":"Move the if-else into a switch expression."},{"id":"d","text":"Create a PaymentProcessorV2 class for each new provider."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q20","text":"ST11-6: Which of the following is a risk of property injection compared to constructor injection?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Property injection is not supported by DI containers."},{"id":"b","text":"Property injection allows the object to be in an invalid state if the property is not set before use, as the dependency is not guaranteed at construction time."},{"id":"c","text":"Property injection is slower than constructor injection."},{"id":"d","text":"Property injection cannot be used with interfaces."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q21","text":"ST01-6: In SRP, 'cohesion' refers to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The number of classes in a module."},{"id":"b","text":"How strongly related and focused the responsibilities of a class or module are."},{"id":"c","text":"The degree to which classes depend on each other."},{"id":"d","text":"The number of public methods a class exposes."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q22","text":"ST18-12: 'Remove Middle Man' refactoring is used when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A class has too many responsibilities and should be split."},{"id":"b","text":"A class does little but delegate every call to another object, adding no value; clients can call the target directly."},{"id":"c","text":"An interface is used only once and should be removed."},{"id":"d","text":"A base class delegates all work to subclasses."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q23","text":"ST07-14: Which of the following best explains why fat interfaces increase coupling?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Fat interfaces use generics, which increases compile time."},{"id":"b","text":"Clients that implement or depend on a fat interface are coupled to methods they never use, meaning changes to unused methods still affect them."},{"id":"c","text":"Fat interfaces cannot be registered in a DI container."},{"id":"d","text":"Fat interfaces prevent polymorphism."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q24","text":"ST19-11: What is the difference between unit tests and integration tests in a SOLID codebase?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Unit tests test multiple classes; integration tests test one class."},{"id":"b","text":"Unit tests isolate a single unit (class/method) using test doubles; integration tests verify that multiple real components work together correctly."},{"id":"c","text":"Integration tests are always slower and less reliable than unit tests."},{"id":"d","text":"SOLID-compliant code only needs integration tests."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q25","text":"ST09-10: A `ReportService` depends on `IDataSource` (interface) instead of `SqlDataSource` (class). If a new `CsvDataSource` is introduced, the `ReportService`:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Must be modified to support CsvDataSource."},{"id":"b","text":"Requires no changes — CsvDataSource implements IDataSource and can be injected without altering ReportService."},{"id":"c","text":"Must be recompiled even though it requires no source changes."},{"id":"d","text":"Cannot use CsvDataSource because it was not anticipated at design time."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q26","text":"ST20-10: A `Product` class exposes 20 public properties and handles pricing, inventory, display, and serialization. Which principle does this most violate?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"ISP — the product interface is too large."},{"id":"b","text":"SRP — a single class handles too many distinct concerns."},{"id":"c","text":"OCP — the product class cannot be extended."},{"id":"d","text":"DIP — the product depends on too many concrete types."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q27","text":"ST10-7: What does 'composition root' mean in the context of dependency injection?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The base class from which all composable services derive."},{"id":"b","text":"The single place in an application (e.g., Program.cs or Startup.cs) where the entire dependency graph is assembled and registered."},{"id":"c","text":"A root interface that all DI-injectable classes must implement."},{"id":"d","text":"The first object created when the application starts."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q28","text":"ST17-15: 'Refused Bequest' is a code smell where:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A class refuses to inherit from a base class."},{"id":"b","text":"A subclass inherits from a parent but ignores or overrides most of the inherited methods, suggesting the inheritance relationship is wrong."},{"id":"c","text":"A class refuses to implement an interface method."},{"id":"d","text":"A class refuses to be injected into other classes."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q29","text":"ST08-11: Which of the following PaymentBase class with virtual methods for all payment operations."},{"id":"d","text":"A static PaymentHelper class with all payment methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s07_q30","text":"ST11-7: Which ASP.NET Core middleware feature is a real-world example of the Chain of Responsibility pattern, related to INSERT INTO papers (id, data) VALUES ('qp_cs_s08', $${"id":"qp_cs_s08","title":"C# SOLID — Paper 8","description":"Covers ISP refactoring, DIP concepts, DIP implementation, SRP concepts, SRP refactoring, Refactoring Techniques, Unit Testing & SOLID, SOLID Real C# Scenarios, Dependency Injection Patterns, and Strategy Pattern.","createdAt":"2026-02-26T09:00:00Z","timer":{"enabled":true,"durationSeconds":2700,"autoSubmit":true},"questions":[{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q1","text":"ST12-7: A `SortContext` class holds a reference to an `ISortStrategy` interface. At runtime, the user selects QuickSort. The correct way to change strategy in C# is:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Instantiate a new SortContext with QuickSortStrategy."},{"id":"b","text":"Call `context.SetStrategy(new QuickSortStrategy())` or pass it via a property/method to swap strategies dynamically."},{"id":"c","text":"Subclass SortContext as QuickSortContext."},{"id":"d","text":"Use reflection to change the private strategy field."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q2","text":"ST01-7: According to SRP, a class with a high 'lack of cohesion of methods' (LCOM) metric suggests:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The class has too few methods."},{"id":"b","text":"Methods in the class do not share instance variables or concerns, indicating the class likely has multiple responsibilities."},{"id":"c","text":"The class is well-designed and focused."},{"id":"d","text":"The class should be made abstract."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q3","text":"ST19-12: Why does a class that uses `new ConcreteService()` internally tend to have poor unit test coverage?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The compiler refuses to compile it without a test."},{"id":"b","text":"The concrete instantiation cannot be replaced with a test double, so tests exercise the real dependency (e.g., a real database or network)."},{"id":"c","text":"Tests cannot import classes that use `new`."},{"id":"d","text":"The DI container rejects it at startup."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q4","text":"ST09-11: Which of the following is a violation of DIP in a repository pattern context?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"An application service accepting ICustomerRepository in its constructor."},{"id":"b","text":"An application service directly referencing `EFCoreCustomerRepository` and calling `new EFCoreCustomerRepository(connectionString)` internally."},{"id":"c","text":"Registering EFCoreCustomerRepository as the implementation of ICustomerRepository in the DI container."},{"id":"d","text":"Defining ICustomerRepository in the domain layer."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q5","text":"ST20-11: A `switch` statement in a method switches on an enum representing shape types to calculate area. A new shape is added and the developer must update the switch. Which principle is violated?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SRP — the method has too many responsibilities."},{"id":"b","text":"OCP — the method must be modified every time a new type is added."},{"id":"c","text":"DIP — the method depends on a concrete enum."},{"id":"d","text":"ISP — the method has too many parameters."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q6","text":"ST08-12: In C#, how does the `IEnumerable<T>` (read-only iteration) vs `IList<T>` (read-write) distinction illustrate ISP?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Both expose the same surface; they are identical from an ISP perspective."},{"id":"b","text":"Methods that only need to iterate should accept IEnumerable<T>, not IList<T>, so callers are not forced to depend on mutation methods they don't use."},{"id":"c","text":"IList<T> should always be used because it is the richer interface."},{"id":"d","text":"IEnumerable<T> violates ISP because it has only one method."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q7","text":"ST11-8: In Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection, a 'captive dependency' occurs when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A singleton service depends on a transient service, potentially holding it alive for the application lifetime."},{"id":"b","text":"A transient service depends on another transient service."},{"id":"c","text":"Two services form a circular dependency."},{"id":"d","text":"A scoped service is not disposed at end of request."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q8","text":"ST18-13: 'Rename Method' refactoring is primarily about:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Changing the method signature to accept different parameter types."},{"id":"b","text":"Improving code readability by giving a method a name that better communicates its intent."},{"id":"c","text":"Splitting a method into two methods with the original name retained."},{"id":"d","text":"Moving a method to a different class."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q9","text":"ST02-7: A class `CustomerService` has three groups of private methods: one group for validation, one for CRUD, and one for notifications. The SRP-appropriate refactoring is to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Keep all methods in CustomerService but prefix them by responsibility."},{"id":"b","text":"Extract CustomerValidator, CustomerRepository, and CustomerNotifier as separate classes and inject them into CustomerService."},{"id":"c","text":"Make all private methods public so they can be tested independently."},{"id":"d","text":"Create a single CustomerServiceHelper class for the private methods."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q10","text":"ST10-8: Which of the following describes the key difference between DI via a container vs. manual DI (Poor Man's DI)?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Containers use reflection; manual DI uses source generators."},{"id":"b","text":"A DI container automates the construction and lifetime management of the object graph; manual DI requires wiring dependencies explicitly in code at the composition root."},{"id":"c","text":"Manual DI is always better because it avoids reflection overhead."},{"id":"d","text":"DI containers enforce SOLID principles; manual DI does not."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q11","text":"ST19-13: When using Moq in C# to verify that a method was called, which syntax is correct?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"mock.Assert(m => m.Send(It.IsAny<string>()))"},{"id":"b","text":"mock.Verify(m => m.Send(It.IsAny<string>()), Times.Once())"},{"id":"c","text":"mock.Expect(m => m.Send(It.IsAny<string>()))"},{"id":"d","text":"mock.Check(m => m.Send(It.IsAny<string>()))"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q12","text":"ST20-12: A C# class `DataExporter` has methods `ExportToCsv()`, `ExportToJson()`, `ExportToXml()`. Each method is 80 lines long. Which SOLID refactoring applies?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Apply DIP — inject the format as an interface."},{"id":"b","text":"Apply OCP via Strategy — create ICsvExporter, IJsonExporter, IXmlExporter (or a single IExporter) and move logic into separate classes."},{"id":"c","text":"Apply ISP — split DataExporter into three interfaces."},{"id":"d","text":"Apply LSP — subclass DataExporter for each format."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q13","text":"ST01-8: Which of the following class names suggests an SRP violation by naming convention alone?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OrderRepository"},{"id":"b","text":"UserManagerAndEmailerAndLogger"},{"id":"c","text":"InvoiceValidator"},{"id":"d","text":"PaymentGateway"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q14","text":"ST12-8: The Strategy pattern aligns with SRP because:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Each strategy class has a single algorithm responsibility, keeping each class focused and testable."},{"id":"b","text":"The context class handles all algorithm selection logic."},{"id":"c","text":"Strategies eliminate the need for inheritance."},{"id":"d","text":"SRP and Strategy are unrelated."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q15","text":"ST09-12: Which of the following correctly implements DIP with constructor injection in C#?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"public class OrderService { private SqlRepository _repo = new SqlRepository(); }"},{"id":"b","text":"public class OrderService { private readonly IOrderRepository _repo; public OrderService(IOrderRepository repo) { _repo = repo; } }"},{"id":"c","text":"public class OrderService { public SqlRepository Repo { get; set; } }"},{"id":"d","text":"public class OrderService { private IOrderRepository _repo => ServiceLocator.Get<IOrderRepository>(); }"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q16","text":"ST18-14: 'Replace Type Code with Subclasses' refactoring is appropriate when:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"An enum has too many values."},{"id":"b","text":"A class uses an integer or enum field to switch behavior in many places; behavior differences can be encoded in subclasses or strategy implementations."},{"id":"c","text":"A type parameter is used only once."},{"id":"d","text":"A class inherits from too many base classes."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q17","text":"ST02-8: 'Separation of concerns' as applied by SRP means:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Each class should be in a separate assembly."},{"id":"b","text":"Different aspects of a problem (e.g., business logic, persistence, UI) should be handled by separate classes, each responsible for its own concern."},{"id":"c","text":"A class should have only one method."},{"id":"d","text":"Concerns should be hidden behind abstract base classes only."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q18","text":"ST11-9: In C#, which interface must a custom DI container's service provider implement to be compatible with ASP.NET Core?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"IServiceFactory"},{"id":"b","text":"IServiceProvider"},{"id":"c","text":"IServiceCollection"},{"id":"d","text":"IDependencyResolver"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q19","text":"ST20-13: Consider: `public void Save(object data) { if (data is Customer c) { SaveCustomer(c); } else if (data is Order o) { SaveOrder(o); } }`. What SOLID issue is present?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"LSP — Save substitutes the wrong type."},{"id":"b","text":"OCP — the method must be modified every time a new data type needs saving; use IStorable interface or overloads instead."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP — the method accepts too broad a type."},{"id":"d","text":"DIP — object is a concrete type."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q20","text":"ST19-14: 'Test isolation' means that a unit test should:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Only be run on an isolated machine."},{"id":"b","text":"Verify one behavior in isolation, using test doubles to remove all external dependencies so failures pinpoint the unit under test."},{"id":"c","text":"Avoid using mock objects."},{"id":"d","text":"Always test the entire system end to end."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q21","text":"ST08-13: ISP and the Facade pattern relate in that:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Facade eliminates the need for ISP by exposing all methods through one object."},{"id":"b","text":"A Facade can present a simplified, role-specific interface to a client while hiding a complex subsystem, serving the same goal as ISP."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP is only applicable when a Facade is present."},{"id":"d","text":"Facade and ISP contradict each other."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q22","text":"ST10-9: Which of the following correctly describes how to register an open generic type in Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"services.AddTransient(typeof(IRepository), typeof(EFRepository))"},{"id":"b","text":"services.AddTransient(typeof(IRepository<>), typeof(EFRepository<>))"},{"id":"c","text":"services.AddTransient<IRepository<>, EFRepository<>>()"},{"id":"d","text":"services.AddGeneric<IRepository, EFRepository>()"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q23","text":"ST12-9: In C#, which of the following is a clean way to implement strategy selection based on runtime input?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A long if-else chain checking a string input for the strategy name."},{"id":"b","text":"A Dictionary<string, IStrategy> keyed by strategy name, returning the appropriate strategy instance for injection."},{"id":"c","text":"Reflection to find all types implementing IStrategy and instantiate them all."},{"id":"d","text":"A switch expression hardcoded to three strategy types."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q24","text":"ST01-9: The SRP principle originated in which broader software engineering concept?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"DRY (Don't Repeat Yourself)"},{"id":"b","text":"Cohesion — specifically the idea of high cohesion within a module, attributed to Larry Constantine and later formalized by Robert C. Martin."},{"id":"c","text":"YAGNI (You Aren't Gonna Need It)"},{"id":"d","text":"CAP theorem"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q25","text":"ST20-14: A developer adds logging to 15 different service classes by copying the same Logger.Log() call. Which code smell and SOLID violation does this represent?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Feature Envy and ISP."},{"id":"b","text":"Duplicate Code and OCP/SRP — logging should be extracted (e.g., as a Decorator or via AOP), not duplicated across classes."},{"id":"c","text":"Shotgun Surgery and DIP."},{"id":"d","text":"Long Method and LSP."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q26","text":"ST18-15: 'Hide Delegate' refactoring is used to:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Make a delegate private."},{"id":"b","text":"Reduce message chains by having a class expose a method that wraps a call to a collaborator, hiding the delegate from the client."},{"id":"c","text":"Replace a delegate with an event."},{"id":"d","text":"Move a delegate field to a base class."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q27","text":"ST09-13: The 'Stable Abstractions Principle' (related to DIP) states that:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A package should be as abstract as it is stable; highly stable packages should be highly abstract."},{"id":"b","text":"Abstract classes should never change once released."},{"id":"c","text":"All interfaces should be in the most stable assembly."},{"id":"d","text":"Abstractions should depend on concrete implementations for stability."}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q28","text":"ST02-9: A `ProductService` class contains: `CalculatePrice()`, `GenerateInvoice()`, `SendConfirmationEmail()`, `SaveToDatabase()`. After SRP refactoring, the correct architecture is:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Move all methods into a ProductHelper static class."},{"id":"b","text":"Extract PricingService, InvoiceService, NotificationService, and ProductRepository, then inject them into a thin orchestrating ProductService."},{"id":"c","text":"Keep ProductService but make all methods virtual for easy overriding."},{"id":"d","text":"Split ProductService into ProductServiceA and ProductServiceB arbitrarily."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q29","text":"ST11-10: In C# ASP.NET Core, `IHttpContextAccessor` is typically registered as which lifetime, and why?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Transient — because HTTP context changes every millisecond."},{"id":"b","text":"Singleton — because the accessor is stateless; only the HttpContext it accesses is per-request."},{"id":"c","text":"Scoped — because it is tied to a specific HTTP request scope."},{"id":"d","text":"Transient — because each service needing it gets a fresh instance."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s08_q30","text":"ST19-15: What is 'test pyramid' and how does SOLID support it?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A pyramid-shaped class hierarchy used in test frameworks."},{"id":"b","text":"A model advocating many fast unit tests at the base, fewer integration tests in the middle, and fewest UI/end-to-end tests at the top; SOLID supports this by making units small, isolated, and mockable."},{"id":"c","text":"A diagram showing code coverage targets."},{"id":"d","text":"A pyramid of abstract base classes used only in test projects."}],"correctOptionId":"b"}]}$$::jsonb) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

fields."},{"id":"d","text":"Only static members."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q24","text":"ST19-18: In the context of SOLID and testing, what is 'seam'?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A be inserted."},{"id":"c","text":"A test that runs at the boundary of two systems."},{"id":"d","text":"A merge conflict in a test file."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q25","text":"ST11-13: HttpClient via reflection for dynamic endpoint support."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q27","text":"ST03-12: Which of the following is an example of OCP being violated in configuration editing the class's source code."},{"id":"c","text":"Using an IOptions<T> abstraction to read configuration."},{"id":"d","text":"Registering multiple implementations of the same interface."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q28","text":"ST20-18: A method `CalculateShipping(Order o)` accesses `o.Customer.Address.Country.TaxRate`. This chain violates:","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OCP — the method cannot be extended."},{"id":"b","text":"Law of Demeter (and indirectly SRP) — the method is navigating deeply into an object graph, becoming tightly coupled to many classes."},{"id":"c","text":"ISP — too many types are accessed."},{"id":"d","text":"DIP — the method depends on concrete classes."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q29","text":"ST02-12: A `PaymentService` class has methods for credit card, PayPal, and bank transfer processing — each with 50+ lines. What SRP + OCP combined refactoring resolves this?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Break the methods into smaller private methods within PaymentService."},{"id":"b","text":"Extract each payment method into a separate class implementing IPaymentProcessor, inject them via DI, and remove the conditional logic from PaymentService."},{"id":"c","text":"Add a PaymentType enum and use a switch expression."},{"id":"d","text":"Create three subclasses: CreditCardPaymentService, PayPalPaymentService, BankTransferPaymentService."}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_cs_s09_q30","text":"ST12-12: Which of the following best describes the relationship between the Strategy pattern and unit testing?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Strategy makes testing harder because there are more classes."},{"id":"b","text":"Each strategy class can be unit tested independently with focused tests, and the context class can be tested with a stub strategy."},{"id":"c","text":"Strategy eliminates the need for unit tests."},{"id":"d","text":"Strategies cannot be mocked."}],"correctOptionId":"b"}]}$$::jsonb) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

statistics, and query hints.","createdAt":"2026-02-26T09:00:00Z","timer":{"enabled":true,"durationSeconds":3600,"autoSubmit":true},"questions":[{"id":"qp_sql_10_q1","text":"What is a query execution plan?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A written document describing database design"},{"id":"b","text":"The sequence of physical operations the query optimizer chooses to execute a SQL query"},{"id":"c","text":"A list of indexes defined on a table"},{"id":"d","text":"The stored procedure cache"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q2","text":"In a graphical execution plan in SQL Server Management Studio, in which direction do you read operators?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Left to right"},{"id":"b","text":"Top to bottom"},{"id":"c","text":"Right to left"},{"id":"d","text":"Bottom to top"}],"correctOptionId":"c"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q3","text":"What does a thick arrow between two operators in an execution plan indicate?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A fast join operation"},{"id":"b","text":"A large number of rows flowing between those operators"},{"id":"c","text":"A high CPU cost operation"},{"id":"d","text":"A network transfer between servers"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q4","text":"What is the difference between an Estimated and an Actual execution plan?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"They are always identical"},{"id":"b","text":"An estimated plan is generated without running the query; an actual plan is generated after execution and includes runtime statistics like actual row counts"},{"id":"c","text":"An actual plan is less accurate than an estimated plan"},{"id":"d","text":"Estimated plans include index usage; actual plans do not"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q5","text":"What does it mean when an execution plan shows a large difference between 'Estimated Rows' and 'Actual Rows'?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The query is running correctly"},{"id":"b","text":"Statistics are likely out of date, causing the optimizer to make poor plan choices"},{"id":"c","text":"The index is corrupted"},{"id":"d","text":"The query is returning too many rows"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q6","text":"What is a Table Scan vs. an Index Seek in execution plans?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Table Scan navigates the B-tree directly; Index Seek reads all pages"},{"id":"b","text":"Table Scan reads every row in the table (no usable index); Index Seek navigates the B-tree to find specific rows efficiently"},{"id":"c","text":"They are equivalent operations"},{"id":"d","text":"Index Seek is only used for full-text search"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q7","text":"What is a Key Lookup (Clustered) in an execution plan, and when is it a performance concern?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It is always efficient and not a concern"},{"id":"b","text":"After a non-clustered index seek, the engine fetches additional columns from the clustered index; it becomes costly when applied to many rows"},{"id":"c","text":"It is a join between two clustered tables"},{"id":"d","text":"It looks up the primary key value in a foreign key table"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q8","text":"What is parameter sniffing and when does it cause problems?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SQL Server detects missing parameters automatically and causes compilation errors"},{"id":"b","text":"SQL Server uses the parameter values from the first execution to compile and cache a plan; this is problematic if later executions use very different parameter values with different optimal plans"},{"id":"c","text":"A security feature that logs parameter values"},{"id":"d","text":"Automatic parameter detection that always improves performance"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q9","text":"Which query hint forces a new execution plan to be compiled for each execution, solving parameter sniffing issues?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"OPTION (OPTIMIZE FOR UNKNOWN)"},{"id":"b","text":"OPTION (RECOMPILE)"},{"id":"c","text":"OPTION (MAXDOP 1)"},{"id":"d","text":"WITH (NOLOCK)"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q10","text":"What does MAXDOP control in query hints?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The maximum number of rows returned"},{"id":"b","text":"The maximum degree of parallelism — the number of CPU cores used for a parallel query"},{"id":"c","text":"The maximum time a query can run"},{"id":"d","text":"The maximum number of joins allowed"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q11","text":"What does OPTION (MAXDOP 1) force?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The query to use all available CPUs"},{"id":"b","text":"The query to run single-threaded with no parallelism"},{"id":"c","text":"The query to use 1 GB of memory"},{"id":"d","text":"The query to recompile once"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q12","text":"What of column values, used by the query optimizer to estimate row counts and choose efficient plans"},{"id":"c","text":"Performance counters for server CPU usage"},{"id":"d","text":"Query execution history logs"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q13","text":"What command updates statistics on a table in SQL Server?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"REFRESH STATISTICS TableName"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q14","text":"What is the difference between a temp table (#temp) and a table variable (@tableVar) in terms of statistics?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Table variables have full statistics; temp tables do not"},{"id":"b","text":"Temp tables have statistics and can be indexed like regular tables; table variables have no statistics and the optimizer always estimates 1 row"},{"id":"c","text":"They are identical in terms of statistics"},{"id":"d","text":"Table variables are always faster than temp tables"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q15","text":"When would you use a temp table over a table variable?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"For small datasets with a few rows"},{"id":"b","text":"For larger datasets where statistics and indexes are needed for subsequent queries, improving optimizer accuracy"},{"id":"c","text":"Table variables are always preferable"},{"id":"d","text":"When the data should persist after the session ends"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q16","text":"What is tempdb used for in SQL internal worktables"},{"id":"c","text":"Storing transaction logs"},{"id":"d","text":"Backing up user databases"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q17","text":"What does the NOLOCK table hint do and what risk does it carry?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Prevents all writes to the table; risk is reduced write performance"},{"id":"b","text":"Reads data without acquiring shared locks, improving read concurrency but risking dirty reads of uncommitted data"},{"id":"c","text":"Locks the table exclusively; risk is blocking other reads"},{"id":"d","text":"Removes all existing locks; risk is data corruption"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q18","text":"What does the READPAST hint do?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Reads all locked rows regardless of locks"},{"id":"b","text":"Skips rows that are currently locked by other transactions instead of waiting"},{"id":"c","text":"Forces the query to read from a past point in time"},{"id":"d","text":"Reads rows in reverse order"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q19","text":"What is the UPDLOCK hint used for?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Prevents the table from being updated"},{"id":"b","text":"Acquires an update lock during a read, signaling intent to update and preventing deadlocks in read-then-update patterns"},{"id":"c","text":"Forces the query to update all rows it reads"},{"id":"d","text":"Bypasses the update trigger"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q20","text":"What is Query Store in SQL Server?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A storage location for creating queries"},{"id":"b","text":"A feature that captures query texts, plans, and runtime statistics over time, enabling plan forcing and regression analysis"},{"id":"c","text":"A cache of recently executed queries"},{"id":"d","text":"A tool for writing stored procedures"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q21","text":"What does 'forcing a plan' in Query Store allow you to do?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Force the query to return specific results"},{"id":"b","text":"Instruct SQL Server to always use a specific previously captured execution plan for a query, bypassing the optimizer"},{"id":"c","text":"Force all queries to run in parallel"},{"id":"d","text":"Force index rebuilds before each execution"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q22","text":"How do you identify blocking queries in SQL Server?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SELECT * FROM sys.indexes"},{"id":"b","text":"Query sys.dm_exec_requests and sys.dm_os_waiting_tasks to find sessions with blocking_session_id populated"},{"id":"c","text":"Use SET STATISTICS IO ON"},{"id":"d","text":"Query sys.databases"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q23","text":"Which DMV can be used to identify the top CPU-consuming queries in SQL Server?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"sys.dm_exec_sessions"},{"id":"b","text":"sys.dm_exec_query_stats ordered by total_worker_time"},{"id":"c","text":"sys.dm_os_performance_counters"},{"id":"d","text":"sys.dm_db_index_usage_stats"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q24","text":"What causes a Hash Match join in an execution plan, and is it always bad?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Hash match occurs on indexed columns only; it is always efficient"},{"id":"b","text":"Hash match is used when joining on unindexed or unsorted columns; it is not inherently bad but can be costly for large datasets due to memory spills to tempdb"},{"id":"c","text":"Hash match always indicates a missing index"},{"id":"d","text":"Hash match only occurs on text columns"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q25","text":"What is a Nested Loops join in an execution plan?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A join algorithm that builds a hash table for one input and probes it with the other"},{"id":"b","text":"A join algorithm where for each row in the outer input, it seeks matching rows in the inner input — efficient when the outer set is small and the inner has a good index"},{"id":"c","text":"A join that sorts both inputs and merges them"},{"id":"d","text":"A join that only works on equal column values"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q26","text":"What is a Merge Join in an execution plan?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"A join that merges two tables into one permanent table"},{"id":"b","text":"A join that requires both inputs to be sorted on the join columns; it then merges them linearly — efficient for large sorted data sets"},{"id":"c","text":"A join that creates a hash table"},{"id":"d","text":"A join used only for FULL OUTER JOIN"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q27","text":"What is a memory spill (worktable spill) in an execution plan and why is it harmful?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"When query results are spilled to a log file — harmful because it fills the log"},{"id":"b","text":"When a sort, hash, or other memory-intensive operation does not fit in granted memory and overflows to tempdb on disk, causing significant I/O overhead"},{"id":"c","text":"When a query uses too many temp tables"},{"id":"d","text":"When a query returns too many rows to the client"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q28","text":"How does OPTION (OPTIMIZE FOR (@param UNKNOWN)) help with parameter sniffing?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"It disables parameter sniffing entirely"},{"id":"b","text":"It instructs the optimizer to use average statistical values rather than the supplied parameter value when compiling the plan, producing a more general plan"},{"id":"c","text":"It forces a recompile on every execution"},{"id":"d","text":"It caches the plan for the unknown parameter type only"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q29","text":"Which sys view in SQL Server helps identify long-running currently executing queries?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"sys.dm_exec_requests joined with sys.dm_exec_sql_text to get query text and start time"},{"id":"b","text":"sys.databases"},{"id":"c","text":"sys.procedures"},{"id":"d","text":"sys.dm_db_index_physical_stats"}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_sql_10_q30","text":"What is the purpose of the DBCC SHOW_STATISTICS command?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Shows the database file sizes"},{"id":"b","text":"Displays the histogram, density vector, and header information for a statistics object on a table, used to diagnose estimation problems"},{"id":"c","text":"Shows the execution plan of a query"},{"id":"d","text":"Displays index fragmentation levels"}],"correctOptionId":"b"}]}$$::jsonb) ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING;

-- paper: sql-dev-01.json
INSERT INTO papers (id, data) VALUES ('qp_sql_d01', $${"id":"qp_sql_d01","title":"SQL Developer — Paper 1","description":"Developer-focused SQL scenarios covering query writing, joins, filtering, aggregations, CTEs, indexes, transactions, window functions, stored procedures, and data safety.","createdAt":"2026-02-26T09:00:00Z","timer":{"enabled":true,"durationSeconds":2700,"autoSubmit":true},"questions":[{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q1","text":"A developer writes: SELECT * FROM Orders JOIN Customers ON Orders.CustomerId = Customers.Id WHERE Orders.Status = 'Pending'. The Orders table has 5 million rows. What is the most immediate performance problem with this query?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SELECT * retrieves all columns including large NVARCHAR and BLOB columns, increasing network I/O and preventing index-only scans"},{"id":"b","text":"The JOIN should be a LEFT JOIN to avoid missing rows"},{"id":"c","text":"The WHERE clause should use a parameterised value instead of a string literal"},{"id":"d","text":"The ON clause column names should be fully qualified with table aliases"}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q2","text":"A reporting query groups sales by region and needs to return only regions where total sales exceed 100000. A developer writes: SELECT Region, SUM(Amount) AS TotalSales FROM Sales WHERE SUM(Amount) > 100000 GROUP BY Region. What is wrong with this query?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SUM cannot be used with GROUP BY"},{"id":"b","text":"Aggregate functions cannot be used in the WHERE clause; HAVING must be used to filter on aggregated values"},{"id":"c","text":"The alias TotalSales cannot be defined in the same SELECT that uses SUM"},{"id":"d","text":"WHERE must appear after GROUP BY in the query"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q3","text":"A developer needs to find all customers who have never placed an order. The Customers table has 500k rows and the Orders table has 3M rows. Which query pattern is most appropriate?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE CustomerId NOT IN (SELECT CustomerId FROM Orders)"},{"id":"b","text":"SELECT c.* FROM Customers c LEFT JOIN Orders o ON c.CustomerId = o.CustomerId WHERE o.CustomerId IS NULL"},{"id":"c","text":"SELECT * FROM Customers EXCEPT SELECT c.* FROM Customers c JOIN Orders o ON c.CustomerId = o.CustomerId"},{"id":"d","text":"SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE CustomerId IN (SELECT CustomerId FROM Orders WHERE CustomerId IS NULL)"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q4","text":"A developer wants to retrieve the top 3 highest-paid employees per department. They write: SELECT DeptId, EmpName, Salary, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY DeptId ORDER BY Salary DESC) AS RowNum FROM Employees. What additional step is needed to complete this requirement?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Replace ROW_NUMBER() with RANK() to handle salary ties correctly"},{"id":"b","text":"Wrap the query in a CTE or subquery and filter WHERE RowNum <= 3"},{"id":"c","text":"Add TOP 3 directly to the outer SELECT"},{"id":"d","text":"Add ORDER BY DeptId to the outer query before applying ROW_NUMBER"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q5","text":"A stored procedure builds a query using string concatenation: SET @sql = 'SELECT * FROM Users WHERE Username = ''' + @Username + ''''. A junior developer claims this is fine because the SP already validates that @Username is not NULL. Why is this still a security vulnerability?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"String concatenation SPs always run slower than inline SQL"},{"id":"b","text":"NULL validation does not prevent a user from passing a value like admin'-- which terminates the string and injects arbitrary SQL"},{"id":"c","text":"The procedure will fail because single quotes cannot appear inside a SET statement"},{"id":"d","text":"Stored procedures cannot use dynamic SQL at all"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q6","text":"An application has a Products table with a non-clustered index on CategoryId. A developer writes: SELECT ProductId, Name FROM Products WHERE LOWER(CategoryCode) = 'electronics'. Why will this query not use the index on CategoryId?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"The index is on CategoryId but the WHERE clause filters on CategoryCode, and wrapping a column in LOWER() also prevents index seeks on that column"},{"id":"b","text":"Non-clustered indexes cannot be used with string comparisons"},{"id":"c","text":"LOWER() is not a valid SQL Server function"},{"id":"d","text":"SELECT must include CategoryId for the index to be used"}],"correctOptionId":"a"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q7","text":"A developer runs the following inside a TRY...CATCH block: BEGIN TRANSACTION; UPDATE Accounts SET Balance = Balance - 500 WHERE AccountId = 1; UPDATE Accounts SET Balance = Balance + 500 WHERE AccountId = 2; COMMIT TRANSACTION. The second UPDATE fails. Without SET XACT_ABORT ON, what happens?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SQL Server automatically rolls back the entire transaction on any error"},{"id":"b","text":"The first UPDATE is committed and the second is silently ignored"},{"id":"c","text":"The transaction remains open and the first UPDATE is uncommitted until the CATCH block explicitly issues ROLLBACK"},{"id":"d","text":"The CATCH block is skipped and the transaction commits with only the first UPDATE"}],"correctOptionId":"c"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q8","text":"A developer needs to identify duplicate email addresses in a Users table before applying a UNIQUE constraint. Which query correctly identifies emails that appear more than once?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"SELECT Email FROM Users WHERE COUNT(Email) > 1"},{"id":"b","text":"SELECT DISTINCT Email FROM Users GROUP BY Email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1"},{"id":"c","text":"SELECT Email, COUNT(*) AS Cnt FROM Users GROUP BY Email HAVING COUNT(*) > 1"},{"id":"d","text":"SELECT Email FROM Users HAVING COUNT(Email) > 1"}],"correctOptionId":"c"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q9","text":"A developer writes a CTE to calculate monthly revenue and then references it twice in the main query — once for current month and once for prior month. A colleague says to use a temp table instead. What is the main technical reason the colleague might be correct?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"CTEs cannot be referenced more than once in a query"},{"id":"b","text":"A CTE is re-evaluated each time it is referenced, so a complex CTE referenced twice performs the work twice; a temp table materialises the result once"},{"id":"c","text":"CTEs do not support window functions"},{"id":"d","text":"Temp tables always use less memory than CTEs"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q10","text":"A developer is adding a composite index on (CustomerId, OrderDate) to speed up a query. The query filter is WHERE CustomerId = 5 AND OrderDate > '2025-01-01'. In what scenario would reversing the column order to (OrderDate, CustomerId) hurt performance?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"If CustomerId has higher cardinality than OrderDate"},{"id":"b","text":"When queries frequently filter on CustomerId alone, the leading column must be CustomerId; reversing the order would make single-column CustomerId filters unable to use the index efficiently"},{"id":"c","text":"SQL Server does not support composite indexes with date columns"},{"id":"d","text":"The reversed index would increase the clustered index size"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q11","text":"A developer uses RANK() OVER (PARTITION BY DeptId ORDER BY Salary DESC) to rank employees. Two employees in the same department have an identical salary of 80000. What rank numbers will they receive?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Both receive rank 1, and the next employee receives rank 2"},{"id":"b","text":"Both receive rank 1, and the next employee receives rank 3"},{"id":"c","text":"They receive ranks 1 and 2 arbitrarily, and the next receives rank 3"},{"id":"d","text":"RANK() does not allow ties and raises an error"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q12","text":"A developer needs to perform a safe bulk delete of archived records older than 2 years. The table has 10 million rows. Which approach is safest and most practical?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"DELETE FROM Archive WHERE CreatedDate < DATEADD(YEAR, -2, GETDATE()) with no WHERE guard"},{"id":"b","text":"TRUNCATE TABLE Archive to remove all rows instantly"},{"id":"c","text":"Delete in batches using a loop: DELETE TOP (5000) FROM Archive WHERE CreatedDate < DATEADD(YEAR, -2, GETDATE()) until @@ROWCOUNT = 0"},{"id":"d","text":"Drop and recreate the Archive table after exporting needed data"}],"correctOptionId":"c"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q13","text":"A developer writes: SELECT CustomerName, OrderDate FROM Customers, Orders. There is no JOIN condition. What is the result?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"An error because SQL requires an explicit JOIN keyword"},{"id":"b","text":"A Cartesian product — every customer row is matched with every order row, producing Customers_count × Orders_count rows"},{"id":"c","text":"Only matching rows are returned, equivalent to an INNER JOIN"},{"id":"d","text":"SQL Server returns NULL for unmatched columns"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q14","text":"A developer needs to compare each employee's salary against their own department's average salary. Which SQL construct is the most straightforward way to achieve this?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Use a correlated subquery in the SELECT clause: (SELECT AVG(Salary) FROM Employees e2 WHERE e2.DeptId = e.DeptId)"},{"id":"b","text":"Use AVG(Salary) OVER (PARTITION BY DeptId) as a window function alongside the regular SELECT columns"},{"id":"c","text":"Create a separate GROUP BY query and join it back to the Employees table"},{"id":"d","text":"Both b and c are correct, but b avoids an extra JOIN and is generally simpler"}],"correctOptionId":"d"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q15","text":"An application issues two concurrent transactions: T1 reads a product's stock count and T2 updates the same row simultaneously. Under READ COMMITTED isolation, what anomaly can still occur?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Dirty reads — T1 reads T2's uncommitted changes"},{"id":"b","text":"Non-repeatable reads — T1 reads the row twice and gets different values because T2 committed between T1's reads"},{"id":"c","text":"Phantom reads are impossible at READ COMMITTED"},{"id":"d","text":"No anomalies occur at READ COMMITTED"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q16","text":"A developer queries a table with a column PhoneNumber that contains NULL for some customers. The query is: SELECT * FROM Customers WHERE PhoneNumber <> '555-0100'. Will rows where PhoneNumber IS NULL be returned?","options":[{"id":"a","text":"Yes, because NULL is not equal to '555-0100'"},{"id":"b","text":"No, because any comparison with NULL yields UNKNOWN, which is treated as false in WHERE clauses"},{"id":"c","text":"Yes, but only if ANSI_NULLS is set to OFF"},{"id":"d","text":"It depends on the data type of PhoneNumber"}],"correctOptionId":"b"},{"id":"qp_sql_d01_q17","text":"A developer is choosing between VARCHAR(255) and NVARCHAR(255) for a customer name column. The application must support international characters including Chinese and Arabic.
